name: typescript-write
description: Write TypeScript and JavaScript code following Metabase coding standards and best practices. Use when developing or refactoring TypeScript/JavaScript code.
capabilities: Provides TypeScript and JavaScript coding guidelines following Metabase standards
Documents ESLint commands for code quality checks
References Prettier formatting commands for code consistency
Documents test commands for JavaScript and ClojureScript
Defines autonomous development workflow with test-driven approach
author: metabase
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown guidelines and JSON metadata. No executable code, network calls, file operations, or cryptographic operations exist in this skill. The static scanner produced false positives by misinterpreting metadata fields as security patterns. All 20 findings are dismissed as the skill poses no security risk.
valueStatement: Developers need consistent TypeScript and JavaScript code that follows project conventions. This skill provides coding guidelines, linting commands, and testing workflows for writing maintainable code in Metabase projects.
limitations: Does not execute code or run commands directly
Does not modify files without explicit user approval
Does not access project files outside the Metabase codebase

useCases.title: Write frontend TypeScript
useCases.description: Create new TypeScript components and modules following Metabase frontend patterns and conventions

useCases.title: Refactor JavaScript code
useCases.description: Modernize legacy JavaScript code to TypeScript with proper type annotations and project standards

useCases.title: Run test suites
useCases.description: Execute targeted unit tests and linting to validate code changes before submission

promptTemplates.title: Add new feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a new TypeScript component following Metabase coding standards. Include proper types, tests, and follow the project development workflow.

promptTemplates.title: Fix bug
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix the TypeScript errors in the codebase. First add a failing test, then implement the fix following Metabase standards.

promptTemplates.title: Refactor code
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this JavaScript file to TypeScript with proper type annotations. Ensure it passes linting and type checking.

promptTemplates.title: Review code
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this code change for Metabase coding standards compliance. Check that linting passes and tests are added for new functionality.

bestPractices: Add failing tests before implementing new functionality to ensure test-driven development
Run linting and type checking continuously during development to catch issues early
Understand existing patterns in the codebase before implementing new features
antiPatterns: Writing code without adding corresponding tests first
Skipping linting checks before committing changes
Implementing features without reviewing existing patterns and conventions

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides guidelines that help AI agents write TypeScript following Metabase standards.

faq.question: What commands are available?
faq.answer: Available commands include yarn lint-eslint-pure for ESLint, yarn prettier for formatting, yarn type-check-pure for TypeScript checking.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my files?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidelines and documentation. It does not execute code or modify files directly.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads its own markdown documentation files. It does not access credentials, environment variables, or make network requests.

faq.question: Why are no tests running?
faq.answer: This skill documents test commands but does not execute them. The AI agent using this skill can run tests when requested.

faq.question: How is this different from other TypeScript skills?
faq.answer: This skill is specific to Metabase coding standards, development workflow, and project-specific commands.
